name: Sigmund
description: Sigmund, a central figure in the Volsunga Saga, is a legendary hero whose life is marked by tragedy, courage, and extraordinary strength. Born into the Volsung clan, descended from Odin himself, Sigmund's early life is marred by the murder of his siblings at a feast orchestrated by King Siggeir. Sigmund, along with his brother Sinfjotli, escapes and avenges their family, demonstrating early signs of his heroic potential. He is best known as the wielder of the sword Gram, which Odin thrust into the Branstock tree, accessible only to Sigmund. His marriage to Borghild is short-lived and ends in her poisoning him. He later marries Hjordis, who is pregnant with his son Sigurd when he is slain in battle against King Lyngvi. Even in death, Sigmund's legacy is powerful; his broken sword Gram is reforged for his son Sigurd, who continues the Volsung lineage and becomes one of the greatest heroes of Norse mythology. Sigmund represents the archetype of the noble warrior, destined for greatness but ultimately felled by fate and betrayal. His story is a cornerstone of Norse heroic literature, embodying themes of vengeance, lineage, and the enduring power of destiny.
subtitle: Bearer of Gram, Father of Sigurd
achievements: Pulled Gram from the Branstock tree
Avenged his siblings' deaths against Siggeir
Survived multiple betrayals and near-death experiences

keyMyths.title: The Sword in the Branstock
keyMyths.description: Odin thrusts a sword into the Branstock tree, declaring that only the strongest can pull it out. Sigmund alone succeeds, claiming Gram as his own, a symbol of his destined heroism.

keyMyths.title: The Vengeance of the Volsungs
keyMyths.description: Sigmund and Sinfjotli escape Siggeir's treachery and spend years living as outlaws, eventually returning to exact a brutal revenge on Siggeir and his family, burning them alive.

keyMyths.title: Sigmund's Death and Sigurd's Birth
keyMyths.description: Fatally wounded by Odin's intervention in battle against Lyngvi, Sigmund dies, but his broken sword Gram is passed to Hjordis, ensuring its reforging and Sigurd's destiny as a hero.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Sigmund and the Volsung clan.
